name: open-cowork
description: Open Cowork - Opensource Claude Cowork for Windows & macOS.

commit.message: Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/dev'
chore: refine settings flow and logger stability
Ignore local workspace artifacts
Polish Claude-inspired supporting surfaces
Refine Claude-inspired UI language
Merge branch 'dev'

feat(ui): new logo, redesigned sidebar with grouped sessions

- Replace app logo with keyboard-hands illustration
- Refactor sidebar: date-grouped sessions (today/yesterday/week/older)
- Add collapsed sidebar view with compact controls
- Add logo image in sidebar header
- Add i18n keys for new sidebar labels (zh/en)
perf: avoid full normalize for config reads
perf: reduce runtime config and session overhead
Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/dev'

fix(ui): wider sidebar, relative time, larger chat title

- Sidebar width w-64 → w-72 for more breathing room
- Add relative time (3m, 2h, 5d) to session list items
- Fix delete button overlapping title text (pr-6 padding)
- Chat title text-base → text-lg for better visibility
Track recent workspace artifacts

fix: trace.update race condition, wire reloadMCP/reloadSandbox, remove stale test

- useIPC: flush pending traces before reading store in trace.update handler,
  preventing stale lookup when trace.step and trace.update arrive in same frame
- index: call reloadMCP()/reloadSandbox() in syncConfigAfterMutation so
  MCP credential and sandbox config changes take effect without app restart
- Delete settings-panel-sidebar.test.ts (sidebar no longer shows tab descriptions)

feat(session): store model per session, simplify sidebar token display

- Add model field to Session type, DB schema (with migration), save/load
- Record model from config at session creation
- Update session.model on each query if config changed, push to renderer
- Sidebar reads session.model instead of global appConfig.model
- Remove token progress bar, keep simple input/output text
- Remove duration timer from session info card
feat: refresh API model presets

feat(ui): redesign right sidebar — session info, token usage, flat layout

- Add session info card: model name, live duration, message count, tool calls
- Add token usage section with progress bar (input/output/total vs 200k)
- Remove Progress section and Tools Used section (redundant with chat area)
- Flatten working directory to one-line with copy/change buttons
- Remove Context fold wrapper, MCP connectors now flat
- Update model presets (Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter/custom)
- Add i18n keys for new panel sections (zh + en)

perf: RAF batching, Zustand selectors, MCP cache, config-aware reload, parallel startup

Phase 1 — Renderer:
- Zustand selector化: all useAppStore() destructures → individual selectors
- React.memo: ContentBlockView, ToolUseBlock, ToolResultBlock, TodoWriteBlock,
  ThinkingBlock, CodeBlock
- RAF-batch stream.partial and trace.step/update in useIPC (1 store update
  per frame instead of 10-20/sec during streaming)

Phase 2 — Config hot-reload:
- Split reloadConfig() into reloadConfig/reloadMCP/reloadSandbox;
  API key changes no longer trigger MCP server re-initialization
- Cache shell env in MCPManager (~500ms saved per server spawn)
- Config fingerprint on initializeServers() — skip when unchanged

Phase 3 — Startup:
- Move createWindow() before heavy backend init
- Parallelize MCP server connections with Promise.allSettled
fix(ui): remove line count from tool output header

fix(ui): merge tool_use + tool_result into single card, fix spinner

ToolUseBlock now searches all session messages for matching tool_result
and displays input+output in one unified card. Spinner only shows while
session has an active turn. Orphan ToolResultBlock is hidden when its
corresponding ToolUseBlock exists in another message.

perf: RAF batching, MCP cache, config-aware reload, parallel startup

Renderer (Phase 1):
- RAF-batch stream.partial and trace.step/update in useIPC — 1 store
  update per frame instead of 10-20/sec during streaming

Config hot-reload (Phase 2):
- Split reloadConfig() into reloadConfig/reloadMCP/reloadSandbox;
  API key changes no longer trigger MCP server re-initialization
- Cache shell env in MCPManager (~500ms saved per server spawn)
- Config fingerprint on initializeServers() — skip when unchanged

Startup (Phase 3):
- Move createWindow() before heavy backend init
- Parallelize MCP server connections with Promise.allSettled

Also: sidebar layout tweak (timestamp on same line as title)

fix(ui): final polish — zero hardcoded colors, heading-serif class, a11y

- Replace all remaining hardcoded Tailwind colors across 4 components:
  red-500 → error, yellow-500/600 → warning (only Titlebar close exempt)
- Extract inline fontFamily to .heading-serif CSS class
- Add title attrs to stop/send buttons (accessibility)
- Unify focus ring opacity to 30 across buttons and inputs
- Fix second appVer Promise type bug in GeneralTab

fix(ui): second-pass polish — chat empty state, welcome centering, color tokens

- Chat: replace plain text empty state with icon + subtle message
- Chat: add title attributes to send/stop buttons
- Chat: extract heading-serif CSS class for serif font
- Welcome: push content above vertical center with bottom padding
- Settings: add truncate to schedule task directory path
- RemoteControl: replace hardcoded red-500/yellow-500 with error/warning tokens
- Nav server: update valid tabs (credentials→removed, language→general)
- Main process: skip single-instance lock in dev mode for clean HMR restarts
- CSS: add heading-serif utility, adjust input focus ring opacity
fix(ui): center settings content area with mx-auto

fix(ui): serif headings, slimmer settings nav, chat empty state polish

- Use Source Serif 4 for welcome title, chat header, and empty state
- Slim down settings sidebar: remove tab descriptions, reduce width
- Increase chat empty state whitespace (py-12 → py-20)
- Increase ContextPanel header padding for breathing room
- Remove unused Languages import

fix(ui): unify shadows, remove hardcoded colors, warm palette tokens

- Replace bg-white with theme tokens (toggle knob, calendar badge)
- Replace bg-black/5 dark:bg-white/10 with bg-surface-active
- Unify all shadow-xl/shadow-2xl to shadow-elevated (6 files)
- Fix SandboxSetupDialog rounded-3xl → rounded-2xl for consistency
- Remove unused accent-light token from tailwind config
- Fix appVersion Promise type bug in SettingsPanel
- Warm color palette: success→sage, mcp→lavender, warning→amber, error→brick

fix(ui): warm color palette, Plus Jakarta Sans font, layout polish

- Replace all hardcoded Tailwind colors (blue-500, green-500, amber-500)
  with semantic theme tokens (accent, success, warning, mcp)
- Warm up functional colors to match terracotta accent:
  success #22c55e → #6b9b76 (sage), mcp #a855f7 → #9a85b8 (lavender),
  warning #eab308 → #c9952a (amber), error #df6666 → #c9584f (brick)
- Switch body font from Inter to Plus Jakarta Sans
- Add max-w-[720px] to settings content area
- Fix welcome page tag clipping (remove overflow-x-auto)
- Soften disclaimer text opacity
- Fix Sidebar search not filtering (filteredSessions was unused)
- Remove unused imports (Search, Chrome)

refactor: remove dead code, unused files, and stale comments

- Delete 4 unused files: cowork-instructions.ts, MCPConnectorsModal.tsx,
  CredentialsModal.tsx, LanguageSwitcher.tsx (never imported)
- Remove unused webdriverio dependency from package.json
- Clean up commented-out code in agent-runner.ts and gui-operate-server.ts
- Remove stale MCPConnectorsModal export from components/index.ts

refactor: use pi-native skill discovery, remove AskUserQuestion/TodoWrite dead code

- Replace systemPromptOverride with appendSystemPrompt + additionalSkillPaths
  so pi's DefaultResourceLoader handles skill discovery and prompt injection
- Remove getAvailableSkillsPrompt (~152 lines), getMCPToolsPrompt, and
  custom skill prompt caching from agent-runner
- Remove AskUserQuestion plumbing across main process: PendingQuestion,
  pendingQuestions, handleQuestionResponse in agent-runner, session-manager,
  index.ts (IPC + remote handlers)
- Remove renderer dead code: pendingQuestion store state, respondToQuestion
  callback, question.request/response IPC types, interactive submit logic
- Keep AskUserQuestionBlock as read-only display for historical sessions
- Clean up TodoWrite from remote-manager safe-tools, i18n keys, stale comments
- Rename agent-runner-plugins.test.ts → agent-runner-pi.test.ts, rewrite tests

fix: harden pi-coding-agent integration — 12 robustness fixes

- Fix empty artifact messages being silently dropped (always clear partial text)
- Handle unknown content block types in message_end (log + pass through)
- Add abort guards to message_update, message_end, tool_execution_end
- Wrap unsubscribe() in try-catch in finally block
- Add 5-minute timeout to piSession.prompt() via AbortController
- Improve MCP tool error capture (instanceof Error check, logError)
- Warn when API key is missing before session creation
- Warn when MCP config fails to load
- Deduplicate error reporting: set alreadyReportedToUser flag in agent-runner
- Fix void initializeMCP() — use .catch() to surface errors
- Fix title generation .catch() for unhandled promise rejection
- Fix one-shot baseUrl override: don't overwrite registry baseUrl with preset
- Fix undefined keyProvider in one-shot title generation
- Add logging to one-shot completeSimple for debugging empty responses

feat(ui): align design system with Claude.ai warm minimal aesthetic

- Theme: accent #f97316→#d97757 (terracotta), backgrounds warmed, error softened
- Tool cards: merge tool_use + tool_result into unified collapsible cards with
  status icons, per-tool icons, duration display, and input/output sections
- Thinking: collapsible card with Brain icon replacing plain italic text
- Typography: add JetBrains Mono for code, Claude-style easing curves
- Layout: input area max-width aligned with message column, consistent px-4
- TracePanel: fix undefined accent-cyan/yellow/etc with theme-aware colors
- ContextPanel: replace hardcoded blue/green/orange with theme vars
- WelcomeView: badge colors now use theme tokens
- Sidebar: close settings on any session click (not just different session)
- Add prefers-reduced-motion support

feat: migrate to pi-coding-agent SDK, fix OpenRouter routing, bridge MCP tools

- Replace Claude SDK + Python proxy with pi-coding-agent as unified backend
- Fix OpenRouter: baseUrl /api → /api/v1, model IDs with dots, force openai-completions via rawProvider
- Bridge MCP tools as customTools for pi-coding-agent (buildMcpCustomTools)
- Fix UI message rendering: handle message_end event for all providers
- Fix API key passing: completeSimple gets apiKey directly, env vars as fallback
- Fix ESM import: async import() for pi-ai in config-store
- Remove dead code: proxy manager, codex runner, openai pipeline, vendor proxy
- Remove obsolete tests for removed modules

refactor(prompt): remove AskUserQuestion and TodoWrite tool guidance

These tools were referenced in the system prompt but never registered
as custom tools in the pi session, so the model couldn't actually call
them. Align with pi-mom's minimal approach: just the 4 built-in coding
tools + MCP tools.

feat(chat): chat-first behavior, compact tool cards, fix artifact detection

- System prompt: default to text replies, only use write/edit when user
  explicitly requests file operations; remove artifact_instructions block
- MessageCard: redesign ToolUseBlock and ToolResultBlock as compact
  single-line collapsed cards (Claude Code style) instead of heavy panels
- Fix trace step ID mismatch: use pi toolCall.id instead of random UUID
  so tool_execution_end can correctly update the corresponding step
- Pass toolInput to trace steps for artifact file path extraction
- Add lowercase 'write'/'edit' to FILE_TOOL_NAMES for pi tool matching

fix(ui): polish design system — theme-aware borders, tighter radius, unified colors

- Replace all hardcoded rgba(255,255,255,...) borders with CSS variable
  --color-border-subtle (fixes light mode invisible borders)
- Widen dark theme color scale for better layer distinction
- Make shadow system theme-aware via CSS variables (dark: stronger + glow, light: subtle)
- Tighten border-radius: lg=8px, xl=10px, 2xl=14px, 3xl=16px
- Reduce prose-chat line-height 1.75→1.625, tighten paragraph/heading spacing
- Fix blockquote hardcoded purple border → theme border color
- Increase input focus ring opacity 10%→25%
- Enlarge sidebar status dots w-2→w-2.5
- Add --color-mcp CSS variable and 'mcp' Tailwind color, replace all
  hardcoded purple-500 across 6 component files
- Standardize animation timing: fade-in 150ms, slide-up 200ms
- Fix pre-existing TS errors in claude-sdk-one-shot.ts and index.ts

fix(ui): auto-collapse sidebar for settings, compact settings nav, tighter tags

- App sidebar auto-collapses when Settings is open, restores on close
- Settings sidebar collapses to icon-only (w-14) below 900px width
- Welcome page tags: tighter padding (px-3 py-2), overflow scroll on narrow

fix(ui): responsive layout, auto-collapse panels, and polish across pages

- Add useWindowSize hook (rAF-throttled) for responsive breakpoints
- Auto-collapse context panel <1100px, sidebar <800px via new store setters
- Settings: shrink sidebar (w-48 lg:w-72), hide tab descriptions on narrow,
  reduce content padding, fix provider/protocol grid cols, fix useEffect
  race condition for nav-server tab switching
- ChatView: responsive header padding/title, message area padding, image grid
- WelcomeView: responsive padding and image grid columns
- Skills tab: line-clamp-2 on long descriptions
- MCP connectors: truncate command/URL overflow with tooltip
- Context panel: truncate working dir path instead of break-all
- Sidebar collapsed: replace cramped text with subtle chevron icon

fix(nav): use executeJavaScript for reliable CLI navigation

Replace unreliable IPC event chain (main→preload→React listener) with
direct executeJavaScript calls to window.__navigate(). Adds timeout,
injection-safe args via JSON.stringify, renderer-readiness check, and
top-level error handling for the async HTTP handler.

docs: add design for pi-coding-agent migration

Replace Claude Agent SDK + Python proxy with pi-coding-agent/pi-ai
for native multi-model support (20+ providers) in pure TypeScript.

feat(nav): add HTTP nav-server for CLI-driven UI navigation

Lightweight HTTP server on 127.0.0.1:19888 that lets CC/Codex
control app page navigation via curl, enabling automated UI/UX review
with the screenshot MCP.

Routes:
  GET /navigate?page=welcome|settings|session&tab=...&id=...
  GET /status

fix(macos): handle SIGTERM/SIGINT for clean Dock exit on pkill

Without signal handlers, pkill sends SIGTERM which bypasses Electron's
before-quit event, leaving orphaned Helper processes and a ghost Dock icon.
Route SIGTERM/SIGINT through app.quit() so cleanup runs normally.

Also wires up nav-server start/stop in the app lifecycle.

perf(ui): memoize MessageCard, reduce observer churn, and add proxy warmup toast

- Wrap MessageCard in React.memo to skip re-renders during streaming
  for all non-streaming messages (biggest win for long conversations)
- Narrow ResizeObserver dep from displayedMessages to .length so
  the observer is not torn down on every partial update
- Reduce MCP server polling from 5s to 30s
- Add proxy.warmup event: show "initializing service..." info toast
  on first launch / provider switch, auto-dismiss on ready
- Fix merge-residual syntax error in preload/index.ts (listPlugins)

fix(skills): use python3 everywhere, remove hardcoded paths, and enrich plugin PATH

- Replace all `python` command invocations with `python3` across pptx, docx,
  xlsx, and pdf SKILL.md/FORMS.md (macOS 12.3+ removed `python` symlink)
- Replace `pip install` with `pip3 install` in pptx SKILL.md dependencies
- Remove hardcoded `skills/public/pptx/` path prefixes from SKILL.md and
  html2pptx.md — paths are now relative to skill directory, resolved via
  system prompt's Skill directory context
- Delete obsolete "Note" paragraph with `find . -name "unpack.py"` fallback
- Enrich PATH in plugin-runtime-service defaultCommandRunner for packaged app:
  macOS/Linux extracts user login shell PATH, Windows uses PowerShell to
  merge User+Machine PATH (consistent with mcp-manager strategy)

fix(macos): keep services alive when window closed via X button

On macOS, window-all-closed was running full cleanup (stopping scheduler,
remote control, sandbox, proxy) even though the app stays in Dock. This
made the app unable to reopen from Dock. Now cleanup only runs on
Windows/Linux quit; macOS defers cleanup to before-quit (Cmd+Q).

feat(ui): convert settings panel from modal to full-page view

- Add showSettings state to Zustand store
- Render SettingsPanel in App.tsx main content area instead of modal overlay
- Remove modal wrapper (fixed/z-50/backdrop) from SettingsPanel
- Use subtle background highlight for active tab instead of accent color
- Close settings when navigating to session or new task
- Fix Electron window recreation on macOS dock click and second-instance

perf(proxy): lock-free warm path, cached resolution, and warmup state API

- ensureReady() checks activeStates directly before entering the
  serialization queue — warm proxy hits skip enqueue() entirely
- resolveVendorRoot() cached at module level; ensurePythonRuntime()
  cached at instance level to avoid repeated fs.existsSync() calls
- warmupForConfig() now exposes _warmupStatus and awaitWarmup() so
  the query path can piggyback on in-progress warmup instead of
  spawning a duplicate proxy
- Add bench-proxy-path.mjs for cold-start + warm-path latency testing

merge(main): integrate main into dev for v3.3.0-beta.1

Merge origin/main into dev preserving all features from both branches:
- Dev: proxy system, scheduled tasks, Codex CLI, plugin V2, API config overhaul
- Main: Windows SendInput, drag-drop attachments, chat layout, icon branding, redaction

Conflict resolution: 21 files resolved (dev architecture as base, main features ported)
Version bumped to 3.3.0-beta.1 (preparing for 3.3 release)

fix(skills): use user's actual shell for PATH and expose skill directory for script resolution

Two issues prevented skills (pptx, docx, etc.) from executing Python/Node
scripts reliably:

1. PATH extraction hardcoded /bin/bash, missing paths only set in the
   user's real shell (zsh, fish). Now uses $SHELL with fish support,
   3s timeout, and broader fallback paths (pyenv, volta, bun, conda,
   Homebrew keg-only node, etc.).

2. Skills reference helper scripts via relative paths (e.g.
   `python scripts/rearrange.py`) but bash CWD is the workspace, not
   the skill directory. Now each skill's absolute directory is included
   in the prompt with explicit path-resolution instructions.
test(merge): add regression tests for proxy, scheduler, and plugin catalog

perf(proxy): eliminate repeated per-query I/O on the hot path

agent-runner.ts
- Extract getBundledNodePaths() to module-level memoized function;
  the two fs.existsSync checks now run once per process lifetime
- Guard the skills directory setup (fs.mkdirSync, readdirSync, statSync,
  symlinkSync) with a _skillsSetupDone instance flag so it runs only on
  the first query; expose invalidateSkillsSetup() for callers that need
  to force a re-run after skill install/remove
- Cache the built mcpServers object keyed by a fingerprint of the
  serialised enabled-server configs + imageCapable flag; subsequent
  queries skip the full getEnabledServers() + loop when nothing changed;
  expose invalidateMcpServersCache() for explicit invalidation

proxy/claude-proxy-manager.ts
- Make release() synchronous: leaseCount decrement is now instant and
  fire-and-forget; only the prune scheduling is enqueued, so the query
  finally block no longer awaits the operation queue

session/session-manager.ts
- Remove unused shouldUseUnifiedClaudeProxy import (pre-existing lint error)

bench: add proxy microbenchmarks and real API latency tool

tests/proxy-perf.bench.ts — vitest bench suite comparing serial vs
parallel port-finding and fixed-250ms vs exponential-backoff health
polling under realistic conditions. Run with: npm run bench

scripts/bench-api.mjs — standalone Node script that decrypts the app
config and fires minimal streaming requests to every configured preset,
reporting TTFT and total latency side-by-side. Run with:
  npm run bench:api
  npm run bench:api -- --set glm --runs 3

package.json — adds "bench" and "bench:api" scripts

perf(proxy): reduce startup and dispatch latency

- Parallelize port availability checks in batches of 8 instead of
  scanning sequentially, reducing port-find time under port pressure
- Replace fixed 250ms health-check polling with exponential backoff
  starting at 50ms (cap 400ms) so a fast-starting proxy is detected
  sooner
- Skip awaiting pruneStaleStates on the ensureReady happy path;
  pruning now runs in the background while the caller receives the
  proxy reference immediately
refactor(schedule): polish time slot editor
feat(schedule): support editable custom time slots
fix(proxy): harden empty response handling
fix(proxy): streamline unified gateway startup
fix(config): preserve duckcoding custom openai models
fix(codex): filter transcript-only agent messages
fix(renderer): lock app viewport scroll

feat(proxy): add unified gateway routing for openai-compatible backends

Add unified Claude/OpenAI proxy routing, config and runtime guards, and vendor the claude-code-proxy runtime.

Also preserve OpenRouter Google Gemini model IDs instead of incorrectly rewriting them to gemini/ aliases.
feat(schedule): unify title generation flow and improve stop control
fix(schedule): unify auto-prefixed titles and stop controls
fix(schedule): prevent enabling overdue one-time task from auto-run
refactor(schedule): improve resume timing and task clarity
fix(schedule): harden trigger flow and task UI state
fix: stabilize API config UX and single-window behavior
Release v3.2.0
Merge branch 'main' of github.com:OpenCoworkAI/open-cowork
feat: update gui operation for windows (wechat summary)
improve key_press to be more robust
improve gui support
refactor(api-config): streamline config set workflow and in-place actions
refactor api config profiles and polish config modal i18n/ui
Merge branch 'main' of https://github.com/OpenCoworkAI/open-cowork
update WeChat QR
fix(mcp): prevent provider env pollution and tighten vision routing
fix(codex): harden vision auth routing and failover handling
fix(context-panel): dedupe and filter artifact file entries
fix(codex): isolate fresh sessions and stabilize local file links
fix(openai): tighten codex auth failover classification

feat(openai): migrate OpenAI flow to Codex CLI with MCP parity

- route OpenAI sessions to Codex CLI by default with responses fallback

- inject Cowork MCP connectors into codex exec and map JSON events to UI trace/tool messages

- harden GUI screenshot+vision flow (structured not-connected reconnect, vision compatibility fixes, cleaner output rendering)

- add backend routing, event-mapper, MCP override, and regression tests for runner/routing/mcp
fix(app): prevent duplicate Electron windows in dev
fix(skills): avoid electron-store projectName typing error
feat(auth): support OpenAI Codex OAuth flow and diagnostic logs
chore(branding): update app icons for packaging
fix(ui): widen chat content area layout
fix(ui): support drag-drop file attachments and bubble layout

feat(plugin): support full V2 plugin runtime and management

- add full plugin catalog/runtime services for skills/commands/agents/hooks/mcp

- wire SDK native plugins injection with runtime materialization and toggles

- add IPC/preload/Settings UI plugin management and success feedback

- keep legacy skills plugin IPC as deprecated compatibility bridge

- add/expand tests for catalog, runtime, runner integration, and UI flows
release: bump to 3.1.0 and harden redaction
fix(agent-runner): define sdkPlugins to avoid runtime ReferenceError
docs(readme-zh): fix notion connector usage sentence
fix(api-test): improve custom anthropic timeout handling
fix: remove hardcoded Chinese text from config modal and titlebar
fix: align packaged app version to 3.0.0
fix: surface Claude stderr on exit code 1 and redact sensitive logs

feat(plugin): support full V2 plugin runtime and management

- add full plugin catalog/runtime services for skills/commands/agents/hooks/mcp

- wire SDK native plugins injection with runtime materialization and toggles

- add IPC/preload/Settings UI plugin management and success feedback

- keep legacy skills plugin IPC as deprecated compatibility bridge

- add/expand tests for catalog, runtime, runner integration, and UI flows
add demo

commit.message: docx: update readme
docx: update readme
Fix GUI dock click targeting and verification gating
Fix remote message content typing for build

releases.name: v3.3.0-beta2
releases.body:
## 更新要点 / Highlights

### 新功能 / New Features
- 统一 Agent 运行时到 `pi-coding-agent` SDK，收敛多 provider 执行链路
- Unified the agent runtime on `pi-coding-agent` SDK with a single execution path across providers

- 定时任务支持单次、每日、每周、多时段等更完整的调度模式
- Expanded scheduled tasks with richer modes including one-time, daily, weekly, and multi-time-slot runs

- 新增定时任务自动标题、立即执行、停止运行和过期保护
- Added auto-generated task titles, run-now support, stop controls, and overdue-task protection

- 插件系统升级，支持插件市场浏览、安装与组件级启停管理
- Upgraded the plugin system with marketplace browsing, installation, and component-level enable/disable controls

### UI/UX
- 重做设置中心，升级为完整页面式配置体验
- Rebuilt the settings center into a full-page configuration experience

- 侧边栏重设计，支持分组会话、搜索和新的品牌视觉
- Redesigned the sidebar with grouped sessions, search, and updated branding

- 右侧信息栏升级，补充 token 用量、产物和最近工作区文件展示
- Enhanced the right-side info panel with token usage, artifacts, and recent workspace files

- 聊天消息与工具卡片样式优化，本地文件链接与产物展示更顺手
- Improved chat and tool card presentation, with smoother local file link and artifact handling

### 优化 / Improvements
- 刷新模型预设与 API 配置流程，增强多模型兼容性
- Refreshed model presets and API configuration flows for better multi-model compatibility

- 优化会话运行、配置读取、MCP 缓存与启动性能
- Optimized session runtime, config reads, MCP caching, and startup performance

- 改进会话级模型保存与切换体验
- Improved per-session model persistence and switching experience

- 优化技能、插件与本地脚本路径解析兼容性
- Improved compatibility for skills, plugins, and local script path resolution

### 稳定性修复 / Fixes
- 修复多项设置流转与单窗口行为问题
- Fixed multiple issues around settings flow and single-window behavior

- 修复 trace 更新、artifact 展示和本地文件链接相关问题
- Fixed issues with trace updates, artifact rendering, and local file links

- 修复定时任务触发、恢复时机和边界状态问题
- Fixed task triggering, resume timing, and scheduling edge cases

- 提升整体运行稳定性，减少多 provider 场景下的异常行为
- Improved overall runtime stability and reduced errors across multi-provider workflows

releases.name: Open Cowork 3.2.0
releases.body:
Release date: 2026-03-02

## Improvements
- Add gui_extract_info in the GUI_Operate MCP server.
- Improve coordinate calculation in GUI operations.

## Fixes
- Fix negative coordinates in cliclick.

releases.name: v3.1.0
releases.body:
## Open Cowork 3.1.0

Release date: 2026-02-15

## New Features

- Added full **Plugin** support end-to-end:
  - plugin catalog integration
  - plugin registry persistence
  - runtime materialization/loading for enabled plugins
  - plugin install/uninstall management flow
- Added plugin-related controls and entries in Settings UI.
- Added dedicated redaction module and test coverage for sensitive log filtering.

## Improvements

- Improved Claude failure diagnostics by surfacing Claude Code stderr context in agent errors.
- Expanded sensitive-data redaction coverage for:
  - API keys
  - auth headers (`Authorization`, `x-api-key`, etc.)
  - bearer tokens
  - query-string tokens (`token`, `access_token`, `api_key`)
- Improved API connection testing for custom Anthropic-compatible providers:
  - timeout increased from 10s to 30s
  - better timeout/network error classification
  - better compatibility behavior when `models.list()` is unsupported
- Improved API settings UX copy and i18n consistency (Config Modal + Titlebar).
- Added compatibility hint text for live-test behavior in Anthropic-compatible scenarios.

## Fixes

- Fixed `ReferenceError: sdkPlugins is not defined` runtime crash in `ClaudeAgentRunner`.
- Fixed packaged app version alignment issues and bumped app version to `3.1.0`.
- Fixed misleading live-test wording by clarifying compatibility-mode behavior.
- Fixed plugin branch integration issues on main during merge (including type compatibility in plugin registry store).

## Validation

- Type check passed: `npx tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- Plugin-related tests passed:
  - `tests/agent-runner-plugins.test.ts`
  - `tests/plugin-catalog-service.test.ts`
  - `tests/plugin-runtime-service.test.ts`
  - `tests/settings-panel-plugin-entry.test.ts`
  - `tests/skills-manager-plugin-install.test.ts`
- Redaction tests passed:
  - `tests/redaction.test.ts`

releases.name: v3.0.0
releases.body:
**New Features**

- Added **Feishu support**, making it easier to integrate Open Cowork into Feishu-based workflows.
- Introduced **remote control capabilities**, including Feishu-integrated remote control flows.
- Added **in-chat screenshot display** plus richer **Artifacts and file link support** for better context sharing.
- Expanded **GUI automation**: more cursor operations, iterative workflows, click history, and Chinese text input.
- Added **Windows GUI support** to improve cross-platform coverage.
- Sessions now get **auto-generated concise titles** for easier history browsing.

**Improvements**

- Improved Artifact and file-link UX in chat: clearer icons, better naming, shorter labels, and cleaner parsing.
- External links now open in the **system browser** for a more consistent experience.
- Remote sessions now appear more quickly after startup.
- Added a **Quartz fallback** when cliclick is unavailable to reduce environment-related failures.

**Fixes**

- Fixed a blank screen issue in remote control settings.
- Fixed missing remote user messages in the UI.
- Fixed remote message content typing issues that caused build failures.
- Fixed multiple MCP and build stability issues (including bundled Node path handling).
- Fixed Node path resolution, Windows packaging/runtime issues, and sandbox environment edge cases.
- Fixed GUI Dock click targeting and verification flow issues.
- Improved grounding accuracy and fixed a Retain-related bug, plus API-call error fixes.

readme_html:
📖 Introduction

Open Cowork is an open-source implementation of Claude Cowork, with one-click installers for Windows and macOS—no coding required.

It provides a sandboxed workspace where AI can manage files, generate professional outputs (PPTX, DOCX, XLSX, etc.) through our built-in Skills system, and connect to desktop apps via MCP (browser, Notion, etc.) for better collaboration.

[!WARNING] Disclaimer: Open Cowork is an AI collaboration tool. Please exercise caution with its operations, especially when authorizing file modifications or deletions. We support VM-based sandbox isolation, but some operations may still carry risks.

✨ Key Features

MCP & Skills Remote Control GUI Operation
Claude Cowork ✓ ✗ ✗
OpenClaw ✓ ✓ ✗
OpenCowork ✓ ✓ ✓
  • One-Click Install, Ready to Use: Pre-built installers for Windows and macOS, no environment setup needed—just download and start using.
  • Flexible Model Support: Supports Claude, OpenAI-compatible APIs, and Chinese models like GLM, MiniMax, Kimi. Use your OpenRouter, Anthropic, or other API keys with flexible configuration. More models coming soon!
  • Remote Control: Connect to collaboration platforms like Feishu (Lark) and other remote services to automate workflows and cross-platform operations.
  • GUI Operation: Control and interact with various desktop GUI applications on your computer. Recommended model: Gemini-3-Pro for optimal GUI understanding and control.
  • Smart File Management: Read, write, and organize files within your workspace.
  • Skills System: Built-in workflows for PPTX, DOCX, PDF, XLSX generation and processing. Supports custom skill creation and deletion.
  • MCP External Service Support: Integrate browser, Notion, custom apps and more through MCP Connectors to extend AI capabilities.
  • Multimodal Input: Drag & drop files and images directly into the chat input for seamless multimodal interaction.
  • Real-time Trace: Watch AI reasoning and tool execution in the Trace Panel.
  • Secure Workspace: All operations confined to your chosen workspace folder.
  • VM-Level Isolation: WSL2 (Windows) and Lima (macOS) VM isolation—all commands execute in an isolated VM to protect your host system.
  • UI Enhancements: Beautiful and flexible UI design, system language switching, comprehensive MCP/Skills/Tools call display.

🎬 Demo

See Open Cowork in action:

1. Folder Organization & Cleanup 📂

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dbeb0337-2d19-4b5d-a438-5220f2a87ca7

2. Generate PPT from Files 📊

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/30299ded-0260-468f-b11d-d282bb9c97f2

3. Generate XLSX Spreadsheets 📉

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f57b9106-4b2c-4747-aecd-a07f78af5dfc

4. GUI Operation🖥

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/75542c76-210f-414d-8182-1da988c148f2

5. Remote control with Feishu(Lark) 🤖

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/05a703de-c0f5-407b-9a43-18b6a172fd74

📦 Installation

Option 1: Download Installer (Recommended)

Get the latest version from our Releases Page.

Platform File Type
Windows .exe
macOS (Apple Silicon) .dmg

Option 2: Build from Source

For developers who want to contribute or modify the codebase:

git clone https://github.com/OpenCoworkAI/open-cowork.git
cd open-cowork
npm install
npm run rebuild
npm run dev

To build the installer locally: npm run build

Security Configuration: 🔒 Sandbox Support

Open Cowork provides multi-level sandbox protection to keep your system safe:

Level Platform Technology Description
Basic All Path Guard File operations restricted to workspace folder
Enhanced Windows WSL2 Commands execute in isolated Linux VM
Enhanced macOS Lima Commands execute in isolated Linux VM
  • Windows (WSL2): When WSL2 is detected, all Bash commands are automatically routed to a Linux VM. The workspace is synced bidirectionally.
  • macOS (Lima): When Lima is installed (brew install lima), commands run in an Ubuntu VM with /Users mounted.
  • Fallback: If no VM is available, commands run natively with path-based restrictions.

Setup (Optional, Recommended)

  • Windows: WSL2 is auto-detected if installed. Install WSL2

  • macOS: Lima is auto-detected if installed. Install command:

brew install lima
# Open Cowork will automatically create and manage a 'claude-sandbox' VM

🚀 Quick Start Guide

1. Get an API Key

You need an API key to power the agent. We support OpenRouter, Anthropic, and various cost-effective Chinese Models.

Provider Get Key / Coding Plan Base URL (Required) Recommended Model
OpenRouter OpenRouter https://openrouter.ai/api claude-4-5-sonnet
Anthropic Anthropic Console (Default) claude-4-5-sonnet
Zhipu AI (GLM) GLM Coding Plan (⚡️Chinese Deal) https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/anthropic glm-4.7, glm-4.6
MiniMax MiniMax Coding Plan https://api.minimaxi.com/anthropic minimax-m2
Kimi Kimi Coding Plan https://api.kimi.com/coding/ kimi-k2

2. Configure

  1. Open the app and click the ⚙️ Settings icon in the bottom left.
  2. Paste your API Key.
  3. Crucial: Set the Base URL according to the table above (especially for Zhipu/MiniMax, etc.).
  4. Enter the Model name you want to use.

3. Start Coworking

  1. Select a Workspace: Choose a folder where Claude is allowed to work.
  2. Enter a Prompt:

    "Read the financial_report.csv in this folder and create a PowerPoint summary with 5 slides."

📝 Important Notes

  1. macOS Installation: If you see a security warning when opening the app, go to System Settings > Privacy & Security and click Open Anyway. If it is still blocked, run:
sudo xattr -rd com.apple.quarantine "/Applications/Open Cowork.app"
  1. Network Access: For tools like WebSearch, you may need to enable "Virtual Network Interface" (TUN Mode) in your proxy settings to ensure connectivity.
  2. Notion Connector: Besides setting the integration token, you also need to add connections in a root page. See https://www.notion.com/help/add-and-manage-connections-with-the-api for more details.

🧰 Skills Library

Open Cowork ships with built-in skills under .claude/skills/, and supports user-added or custom skills, including:

  • pptx for PowerPoint generation
  • docx for Word document processing
  • pdf for PDF handling and forms
  • xlsx for Excel spreadsheet support
  • skill-creator for creating custom skills

🏗️ Architecture

open-cowork/
├── src/
│   ├── main/                    # Electron Main Process (Node.js)
│   │   ├── index.ts             # Main entry point
│   │   ├── claude/              # Agent SDK & Runner
│   │   │   └── agent-runner.ts  # AI agent execution logic
│   │   ├── config/              # Configuration management
│   │   │   └── config-store.ts  # Persistent settings storage
│   │   ├── db/                  # Database layer
│   │   │   └── database.ts      # SQLite/data persistence
│   │   ├── ipc/                 # IPC handlers
│   │   ├── memory/              # Memory management
│   │   │   └── memory-manager.ts
│   │   ├── sandbox/             # Security & Path Resolution
│   │   │   └── path-resolver.ts # Sandboxed file access
│   │   ├── session/             # Session management
│   │   │   └── session-manager.ts
│   │   ├── skills/              # Skill Loader & Manager
│   │   │   └── skills-manager.ts
│   │   └── tools/               # Tool execution
│   │       └── tool-executor.ts # Tool call handling
│   ├── preload/                 # Electron preload scripts
│   │   └── index.ts             # Context bridge setup
│   └── renderer/                # Frontend UI (React + Tailwind)
│       ├── App.tsx              # Root component
│       ├── main.tsx             # React entry point
│       ├── components/          # UI Components
│       │   ├── ChatView.tsx     # Main chat interface
│       │   ├── ConfigModal.tsx  # Settings dialog
│       │   ├── ContextPanel.tsx # File context display
│       │   ├── MessageCard.tsx  # Chat message component
│       │   ├── PermissionDialog.tsx
│       │   ├── Sidebar.tsx      # Navigation sidebar
│       │   ├── Titlebar.tsx     # Custom window titlebar
│       │   ├── TracePanel.tsx   # AI reasoning trace
│       │   └── WelcomeView.tsx  # Onboarding screen
│       ├── hooks/               # Custom React hooks
│       │   └── useIPC.ts        # IPC communication hook
│       ├── store/               # State management
│       │   └── index.ts
│       ├── styles/              # CSS styles
│       │   └── globals.css
│       ├── types/               # TypeScript types
│       │   └── index.ts
│       └── utils/               # Utility functions
├── .claude/
│   └── skills/                  # Default Skill Definitions
│       ├── pptx/                # PowerPoint generation
│       ├── docx/                # Word document processing
│       ├── pdf/                 # PDF handling & forms
│       ├── xlsx/                # Excel spreadsheet support
│       └── skill-creator/       # Skill development toolkit
├── resources/                   # Static Assets (icons, images)
├── electron-builder.yml         # Build configuration
├── vite.config.ts               # Vite bundler config
└── package.json                 # Dependencies & scripts

🗺️ Roadmap

  • Core: Stable Windows & macOS Installers
  • Security: Full Filesystem Sandboxing
  • Skills: PPTX, DOCX, PDF, XLSX Support + Custom Skill Management
  • VM Sandbox: WSL2 (Windows) and Lima (macOS) isolation support
  • MCP Connectors: Custom connector support for external service integration
  • Rich Input: File upload and image input in chat
  • Multi-Model: OpenAI-compatible API support (iterating)
  • UI/UX: Enhanced interface with English/Chinese localization
  • Memory Optimization: Improved context management for longer sessions and cross-session memory.
  • New Features: Stay tuned!

🛠️ Contributing

We welcome contributions! Whether it's a new Skill, a UI fix, or a security improvement:

  1. Fork the repo.
  2. Create a branch (git checkout -b feature/NewSkill).
  3. Submit a PR.

💬 Community

Join our WeChat group for support and discussion:

MIT © Open Cowork Team
